Non vi è presente la Dashboard di instantink all’interno della app (e questo lo trovo un grande limite e sarebbe facilissimo da sviluppare). 
Aggiornamenti della app e dei firmware delle stampanti avvengono davvero di rado e questa è una grande falla della società che se ne frega della sicurezza degli utenti e del supporto dei prodotti, pensando solo al profitto sui servizi (che sono progettati malissimo). NON CI SIAMO!

author: EbonyAngel1
title: HP Printers—2nd to None!
content: Today I installed my new HP Envy Photo 7260r. It was almost too easy. HP has been my choice for the last 15 to 20 years, and there is no way I’d change. (I also have an HP Envy 5660 that just won’t quit. I’ll be donating it soon.) I’ve tried/used several other brands over the years, but none comes close to the ease of using an HP.  As stated setup is amazingly easy. Scanning is a breeze; the print quality is outstanding. Saving a document to a pdf from the print options is truly my favorite feature. Remote access is fantastic. author: Neeluljhu
title: Ultra pénible pour les nerfs
content: Eh bien je vais laisser mon avis ! Si l’imprimante en soi (Envy) est bien meilleure que mon ancienne deskjet, la connection est un enfer et je me retiens souvent de ne pas taper un bon coup de poing dessus ou la jeter par la fenêtre ! Déjà, l’imprimante ne se met jamais vraiment en veille : cette nouille met des messages chaque fois que j’arrête le partage de connection mobile et reste allumée visiblement toute la nuit avec son message idiot. Par contre elle ne se reconnecte jamais automatiquement quand je remets le partage de données et que je veux imprimer 😬 et cet après-midi elle n’a pas imprimé toutes les pages souhaitées et… ne fait pas le travail bien que la connection soit là et que je relance une impression. En gros, ras le bol du binz HP ! Même les informations sur mon abonnement instant ink sont fausses et je déteste aussi le contrôle effectué par HP qui bloque toute impression si je ne suis pas connectée quelques jours (après des années de bon fonctionnement avec le même abonnement.

title: Everyone Hates Printers, and it’s HP’s fault
content: Printers have always been hated, but they used to have more hardware issues than software. Every time my mom needs to print or scan something this software is the issue. Then after complying to that privacy invasion, I am told “printer not responding” “scanner unavailable” and the solution is to restart? Anyone involved in planning, coding, and/or promoting this app should go on strike, and then just quit, for the betterment of society. If you rated this app above 2 stars, please include how much they are paying you. I’m so relieved that my generation never uses scanners or printers, I am excited for this company to go bankrupt and die. Shout out to the movie Office Space, I hate everything about printers and it’s their fault.

author: Bleepless in Bellingham
title: Genuine HP ink cartridge not good enough for HP
content:
Bought a genuine HP ink cartridge, I used it to print a couple pages, and HP suddenly bricked it for being “HP Protected on another printer.” Clearly some sort of malfunction, but a pretty ridiculous “protection” feature, HP. And now I’ve lost half an hour troubleshooting this nonsense.

So, let’s get this straight—if I had purchased HP ink and my printer broke, and I bought another HP printer to use that same ink cartridge in (because the ink cartridges cost almost as much as a new printer, and why would I want to trash a brand new ink cartridge?), HP would refuse to use that cartridge because another HP printer had used it? That doesn’t “protect” anything but HP’s profits. 

Fellow consumers, when you think corporate greed, think HP. Gross way to treat your customers. Wasteful way to treat your product. Is it worth degrading yourself like this so you could pay your CEO $23 million dollars last year?

author: amjice
title: iOS App (Updated)
content:
App keeps getting better and better: the ability to select a 2-sided scan is great, thank you!

While the laptop version of the app is more robust, and understandably so,at I request that a 600 DPI scan control option be made available from within the app?

Firstly, the iOS app is far more convenient to control the scanner than it is to boot up the Windows laptop and use the more robust control interface.

Secondly, more and more travelers and security professionals recommend saving a digital copy in a folder structure in iOS and 600 DPI seems to be the norm for important documents like passports, driver licenses, birth certificates, etc. to capture the fine security details and other official imprints. 

It would only make sense, then, to use the iOS app and save directly into the iPhone / iPad than to separately boot up a laptop and then have to transfer it to the smartphone.

Thanks for your consideration of this important, often overlooked feature!

OLD: Would be great if the iOS Smart app can scan _both_sides_ from the document feeder like it already can using the Windows version of the app.  I can't imagine that would be a difficult feature to make available.
